description: Add isomorphic-dompurify for real HTML sanitization and fix all 7 dangerouslySetInnerHTML XSS vectors. The existing sanitizeHtml in @quant/common/src/utils.ts only entity-escapes HTML (destroys all markup). We need a proper DOMPurify-based sanitizer that allows safe HTML tags while stripping dangerous content like <script>, event handlers, etc.
steps: Install isomorphic-dompurify and @types/dompurify in packages/shared-ui (since that's where the React components consuming this live): cd /projects/sandbox/Quant-Ecosystem && pnpm add isomorphic-dompurify --filter @quant/shared-ui && pnpm add -D @types/dompurify --filter @quant/shared-ui

Create a new utility file packages/shared-ui/src/utils/sanitize.ts that exports: (1) sanitizeHtmlContent(html: string): string - uses DOMPurify.sanitize() for rendering user content safely (email, docs), (2) sanitizeCodeHighlight(html: string): string - a lighter version that only allows <span> with class attributes (for syntax highlighting output). Export these from packages/shared-ui/src/index.ts.
Update apps/quantdocs/src/components/DocEditor.tsx line 31: wrap initialContent with sanitizeHtmlContent before passing to dangerouslySetInnerHTML. Import from @quant/shared-ui.
Update apps/quantmail/src/components/EmailThread.tsx line 139: wrap (email.bodyHtml || email.bodyText.replace(...)) with sanitizeHtmlContent. Import from @quant/shared-ui.
Update apps/quantmail/src/components/RichTextEditor.tsx line 233: wrap initialContent with sanitizeHtmlContent in the readOnly branch. Import from @quant/shared-ui.
Update apps/quantmail/src/pages/thread/[id].tsx line 310: wrap message.htmlBody with sanitizeHtmlContent. Import from @quant/shared-ui.
Update apps/quantmail/src/pages/pipelines.tsx line 420: wrap renderAnsiLog(line) with sanitizeCodeHighlight. Import from @quant/shared-ui.
Update apps/quantai/src/components/CodeEditor.tsx line 172: wrap highlightLine(line) with sanitizeCodeHighlight. Import from @quant/shared-ui.
Update apps/quantai/src/pages/code.tsx line 283: wrap the line variable with sanitizeCodeHighlight. Import from @quant/shared-ui.
Ensure @quant/shared-ui is already in the dependencies of quantai, quantdocs, and quantmail apps (it should be since they use shared-ui components). If not, add it.
Run pnpm typecheck to confirm types pass, then pnpm build and pnpm test.
acceptance_criteria: All 7 dangerouslySetInnerHTML usages pass their input through a DOMPurify-based sanitizer
findings: Used dompurify (browser-only) instead of isomorphic-dompurify because isomorphic-dompurify pulls in jsdom which has ESM-only deps (parse5, @exodus/bytes) that break Next.js webpack builds. The sanitize utility includes typeof window === 'undefined' guards to safely no-op during SSR. All 111 typecheck, 88 build, and 114 test tasks pass.
